message:
Add verify flag and improve UX in PowerShell installer

Enhance install.ps1 to match bash installer capabilities with self-test
support, condensed error handling, and improved PATH guidance for all modes.

What changed:
- Added -Verify switch parameter to run self-test after installation
  * When enabled, executes 'coding-agent-search.exe --version' and displays
    output to confirm binary is functional
  * Mirrors --verify flag from install.sh for cross-platform consistency
- Condensed error handling blocks into single-line statements with compact
  if-then syntax for better readability
- Improved PATH guidance: now shows "Add $Dest to PATH" message even in
  non-easy mode (previously only in EasyMode)
- Simplified checksum URL resolution logic with compact if-block syntax

Technical details:
- Checksum verification flow: explicit -Checksum > -ChecksumUrl > auto-fetch
  ${url}.sha256 (mirrors bash behavior)
- Error messages maintain fail-closed semantics ("refusing to install" on
  checksum issues)
- Uses Write-Host for --version output when -Verify flag is set
- PATH modification in EasyMode still uses SetEnvironmentVariable for User scope

UX improvements:
- Self-test flag provides immediate feedback that installation succeeded and
  binary is executable
- PATH guidance now consistent across both EasyMode and normal mode (EasyMode
  modifies automatically and reports it, normal mode advises user)
- More concise error handling reduces visual clutter without sacrificing safety

Safety preserved:
- Checksum verification still mandatory (fail-closed)
- $ErrorActionPreference = "Stop" ensures any error halts execution
- Temp directory cleanup via automatic PowerShell scope disposal
- No destructive operations on user data

This change aligns install.ps1 with install.sh feature parity for the --verify
flag and improves the overall installation experience on Windows.
